title: ActiveMQ Advisory Message Storm Detection
dataSources: `index=messaging` `sourcetype=activemq:web` Jetty/HTTP access logs for the broker’s web console or REST, or `sourcetype=activemq:log` when advisory consumers are enabled and logged
app: Universal Forwarder on Jetty request log; optional custom logging of advisory consumers
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="activemq:web" earliest=-4h
| search uri_path="*Advisory*" OR uri_path="*advisory*" OR request_uri="*ActiveMQ.Advisory*"
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as advisory_http_hits by _time, host, client_ip
| eventstats median(advisory_http_hits) as med by host
| where advisory_http_hits > med*5 AND advisory_http_hits > 500
| table _time, host, client_ip, advisory_http_hits, med
description: Advisory topics carry connection and destination lifecycle events; a buggy client or misconfigured bridge that churns subscriptions can generate huge advisory traffic and CPU load. Spikes in HTTP access to `ActiveMQ.Advisory` paths or console polling often precede broker saturation.
value: Protects shared brokers from self-inflicted advisory storms that look like organic traffic growth until thread dumps show advisory fan-out.
implementation: Ensure Tomcat/Jetty access logs include `uri_path` or `request_uri`. Baseline per environment; tune multiplier and floor. For log-only sites, mirror with `activemq:log` searches on `Advisory`.
visualization: Line chart (advisory HTTP hits), table (client_ip, count), anomaly overlay vs median.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for unusual bursts of internal notification traffic that can overwhelm the messaging server when something is reconnecting or subscribing in a tight loop.
